evals.prompt: The create-changelog custom skill was deleted from .claude/skills/ but is still referenced in README.md and AGENTS.md. Run the align-skills-documentation skill and verify it removes all references to create-changelog from both files. The skill should automatically detect that create-changelog SKILL.md no longer exists and clean up the documentation.
evals.expected_output: README.md and AGENTS.md updated to remove create-changelog entries. Report shows create-changelog in 'removed' section with details about what was cleaned up.

assertions.name: create_changelog_removed_from_readme_table
assertions.description: Verify create-changelog row is removed from skills table in README.md

assertions.name: create_changelog_removed_from_agents_table
assertions.description: Verify create-changelog row is removed from AGENTS.md custom skills section

assertions.name: create_changelog_not_in_directory_listing
assertions.description: Verify create-changelog is not in README.md directory structure listing

assertions.name: report_lists_removed_skill
assertions.description: Report should show create-changelog in 'removed' or 'orphaned' section with count

evals.prompt: Run the align-skills-documentation skill on a project where all custom skills exist (pr, github-mcp-setup, align-skills-documentation, semantic-release, git-branch). Verify the skill correctly discovers all existing custom skills and doesn't accidentally remove or modify valid documentation. The report should show zero removed skills.
evals.expected_output: All existing custom skills properly documented. No spurious removals. Report shows all custom skills present with accurate metadata and 'Custom' type.

assertions.name: all_custom_skills_discovered
assertions.description: All five custom skills (pr, github-mcp-setup, align-skills-documentation, semantic-release, git-branch) are discovered

assertions.name: custom_skills_marked_correctly
assertions.description: All custom skills are marked with type='Custom' not 'Open-source'

assertions.name: existing_skills_preserved
assertions.description: All existing custom skills remain in README.md skills table

assertions.name: no_false_removals
assertions.description: Report shows zero removed skills (no false positives)

evals.prompt: The project has both frontend-design and skill-creator from skills-lock.json (open-source from anthropics/skills) AND custom skills like pr and github-mcp-setup. Run the align-skills-documentation skill and verify: (1) frontend-design and skill-creator are marked as 'Open-source' type with source='anthropics/skills', (2) pr and github-mcp-setup are marked as 'Custom' type with local SKILL.md paths, (3) no skill is marked as both types. This tests that discovery correctly prioritizes skills-lock.json sources.
evals.expected_output: Skills correctly categorized: frontend-design and skill-creator as Open-source, pr and github-mcp-setup as Custom. No type conflicts or duplicate entries.

assertions.name: opensource_skills_correctly_identified
assertions.description: frontend-design and skill-creator marked as Open-source type from anthropics/skills

assertions.name: custom_skills_correctly_identified
assertions.description: pr, github-mcp-setup, semantic-release marked as Custom type with .claude/skills paths

assertions.name: no_type_conflicts
assertions.description: No skill appears with conflicting types or source attributions

assertions.name: source_attribution_correct
assertions.description: Open-source skills link to 'anthropics/skills', Custom skills link to local paths

evals.prompt: A project documentation has three non-existing skills referenced in docs: 'old-tool', 'deprecated-helper', 'removed-workflow'. Actual available skills: pr, github-mcp-setup, align-skills-documentation. Run the skill and verify: (1) removes all three non-existing skills from README and AGENTS.md, (2) discovers and documents only the three actual skills as Custom type, (3) generates detailed report showing what was cleaned up.
evals.expected_output: All three non-existing skills removed from both files. Only three actual skills documented as Custom. Clean audit report listing removed skills.

assertions.name: all_orphaned_skills_removed
assertions.description: All three non-existing skills completely removed from README and AGENTS.md

assertions.name: only_existing_skills_documented
assertions.description: Only the three actual skills appear in documentation

assertions.name: no_orphaned_references_remain
assertions.description: No lingering references to removed skills in any section (table, integration guide, directory)

assertions.name: cleanup_report_complete
assertions.description: Report accurately lists all three removed skills with what was cleaned up

evals.prompt: The project has: (1) Custom skills in .claude/skills/ (semantic-release, pr, git-branch), (2) Open-source skills in skills-lock.json (frontend-design, skill-creator), (3) Agent skills from enabled agent-skills plugin (spec, plan, build, test, review), (4) orphaned references to 'old-skill' in docs. Run align-skills-documentation and verify correct discovery and categorization of all skill types, with only one entry per skill regardless of source.
evals.expected_output: All skill types correctly discovered and categorized. No duplicate entries. Orphaned 'old-skill' removed. Report shows accurate counts: 3 Custom, 2 Open-source, 5 Agent Skills.

assertions.name: custom_skills_counted_correctly
assertions.description: Exactly 3 custom skills discovered and marked as Custom type

assertions.name: opensource_skills_counted_correctly
assertions.description: Exactly 2 open-source skills discovered and marked as Open-source type

assertions.name: agent_skills_counted_correctly
assertions.description: At least 5 agent skills discovered and marked as Agent Skill type

assertions.name: no_duplicates_across_sources
assertions.description: No skill appears multiple times in discovered skills with different types

assertions.name: orphaned_removed
assertions.description: Orphaned 'old-skill' is removed from documentation
